title: Training Dummy / NPC
description: This Projects adds Trainings Dummies / NPC per command. You can see how much damage you deal and customzie pretty much everything :)
body:
# ‼️INFO INFO‼️:

### ‼️Requires support mods‼️: 
**[Fabric Language Kotlin](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-language-kotlin)** and **[Fabric API](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-api)**

### Need Help? Join my Discord: 
https://discord.gg/SewVJrq5jk

# TL;DR 📑:

- /dummy create <type> - Spawns a dummy (e.g. zombie, skeleton).
- **/dummy player	- Spawns a player for PvP testing. (Enable ARMOR, SHIELD OR TOTEM with shift / sneak + right-click** ⚡
- /dummy info	- In-game guide for all features.
- /dummy remove - Deletes your currently selected dummy.
- /dummy delete -	Deletes ALL dummies in the world. ❌
- /dummy clear-indicators	- Clears all floating damage text. (bug - will be fixed soon)
- Requires support mods: Fabric Language Kotlin & Fabric API
# Description:
Test your weapons, skills, and abilities like a pro with Damage Dummy Mod! 🗡️ Spawn fully configurable dummy entities which you can customize with nearly everything  like blocking shield, totem or armor. Perfect for map creators, server admins, or anyone who loves experimenting with combat mechanics. ⚔️

# Key Features:

1. Shift-Click Editor: Shift + Right-Click a selected dummy to open a GUI menu where you can toggle all settings instantly! ⚡
 
2. Selection System: Simply Right-Click a dummy to select it. It will glow for 15 seconds, allowing you to execute commands or open the editor. ✨

3. Custom Options – /dummy <commands> lets you:

      - Toggle knockback 🌀 (works even if AI is off)
      - Enable or disable AI 🤖

# Important Features

- Damage Indicators – Floating heart indicators 💖 show exactly how much damage your attacks    deal.
- Persistent Selection – Selected dummies glow 🌟 so you can easily track them.

# Why Use This Mod?

Perfect for testing damage, mechanics, or weapon balance ⚔️.

Full control over dummy behavior, even with AI disabled 🤩.

Lightweight, easy-to-use commands—no setup required ⚡.
